# Appendices

<span class="part-kicker">Reference</span>

The appendices are working documents, not decorative back matter. Use them when you need a definition, checklist, template, review structure, or interview preparation aid.

## What Is Here

<section class="part-card-grid">
  <article class="part-card">
    <h3>Reference</h3>
    <p>Glossary and acronyms for terms that recur across ML, LLMs, backend engineering, identity, governance, and delivery.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="part-card">
    <h3>Architecture and Delivery Templates</h3>
    <p>Architecture Decision Record (ADR), AI intake, AI lead response, High-Level Design (HLD), Low-Level Design (LLD), and sprint planning templates.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="part-card">
    <h3>Review Checklists</h3>
    <p>Security review, model evaluation, RAG evaluation, launch readiness, data contract, risk register, and post-launch review.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="part-card">
    <h3>Career Preparation</h3>
    <p>Interview preparation notes and further reading for expanding the starter book into deeper expertise.</p>
  </article>
</section>

## Recommended Use

Do not wait until the end of the book to use these materials. When a chapter introduces a decision or review gate, open the corresponding appendix and fill in a small version for the document Q&A assistant.
